than the man's risk of having breast cancer. Which of the following BEST
describes this observation?
explanation: The correct answer is C.

question_text:
A woman is heterozygous for a disease-causing variant in the Glucose6- Phosphate-Dehydrogenase (G6PD) gene. When her blood G6PD
enzyme was measured, her value was 0.5 (normal range is 5.5 to 20.5
µmol/min/ml). Which of the following BEST explains this observation?
explanation: The correct answer is A.

question_text:
A man has just been diagnosed to have MELAS (mitochondrial
encephalomyopathy with lactic acidosis and stroke-like episodes)
syndrome. His family history suggests that his mother was affected, his
mother's sister was affected, and his mother's brother was affected. H
story also suggests that about half of his siblings are affected. This man
has 2 young children who are currently well. He is worried that his
children will also have MELAS. What is the probability that they will have
symptoms of MELAS?

options.text: None as they have no probability of inheriting it from him
None as they have no symptoms; this means they did not inherit it
50% as the family history is suggestive of a dominant condition
25% as the family history is suggestive of a recessive condition
Further genetic testing will need to be done on the children before we can ascertain their risk

explanation: The correct answer is A.

question_text:
The world's distribution of alpha-thalassaemia carriers is shown (see
attached figure). What is the MOST LIKELY cause for this pattern of
distribution?
explanation: The correct answer is B.

question_text:
You are considering prescribing tetracycline to a pregnant lady as she is
having an infection. You are unsure if it is safe. Which of the following
actions would provide you with the MOST accurate information?
explanation: The correct answer is D.

question_text:
A 30-year-old man comes to see you as he wants you to help him with
genetic testing. He has an autosomal dominant condition called
Huntington Chorea running in his family. His father and his paternal uncle
both have it. He is currently well but he is worried about his health. What
is this man asking for?
explanation: The correct answer is B.

question_text:
A 22-year-old man had genetic testing done and he is diagnosed to have
classic Familial Adenomatous Polyposis. He has come to see you for a
second opinion. He wants to know what his life-time risk is of having
colon cancer.
What is the MOST appropriate answer?
explanation: The correct answer is E.

question_text:
The Expanded Newborn Screening Programme has been further expanded in
October 2019 to cover the following conditions, EXCEPT:
explanation: The correct answer is E.

question_text:
In Singapore, up to 30% of women have diabetes mellitus during the later part
of pregnancy (gestational diabetes). If a woman has poorly controlled diabetes
during the last 3 months of her pregnancy, what is the newborn baby MOST at
risk of?
explanation: The correct answer is A.

question_text:
A 43-year-old healthy woman is currently pregnant. Her husband is 38years old and healthy. They are not consanguineous. There is no family
history of note. Which of the following is the foetus MOST at risk of?
explanation: The correct answer is A.

question_text:
An 8-year-old child has developed epilepsy and needs treatment. Her HLAB*1502 test is positive. Which of the following drugs MUST be avoided in
her case?
explanation: The correct answer is A.

question_text:
A man has neurofibromatosis type 1, and he is the first to be affected in his
family. His symptoms only started when he was 10 years old. The NF1
gene is the gene involved in the pathogenesis of neurofibromatosis type
1. Genetic testing confirmed that he is heterozygous for a diseasecausing variant in the NF1 gene. He asked when the mutation occurred.
What is the MOST APPROPRIATE answer?

options.text: At the formation of the sperm or egg

explanation: The correct answer is A.

question_text:
Drug X has an oral bioavailability of 10%. The low bioavailability of Drug X is
independent of which of the following properties of Drug X?

options.text: Drug X is a good substrate for p-glycoprotein
Drug X has a hepatic extraction ratio of more than 0.7
Drug X is extensively metabolized in the enterocytes

explanation: The correct answer is B.

question_text:
Drug X has a Vd of 5 L, and the elimination half-life of the drug is 12 hours. The
minimum effective concentration is 10 mg/L. A patient is taking 50 mg of Drug
X once a day. Which of the following statements BEST describes the effect of
changes on the steady state concentration of Drug X?

options.text: When the regimen is changed to 50 mg twice a day, the steady state concentration will be reached in approximately 60 hours
When the dose is increased to 100 mg once a day, the steady state concentration will be reached in approximately 30 hours
When the dose is increased to 100 mg once a day, the steady state concentration will decrease
When the regimen is changed to 50 mg twice a day, the steady state concentration will decrease
When the total clearance of the drug is increased, the steady state concentration will be reached in a longer time

explanation: The correct answer is A.

question_text: Drug distribution is independent of which of the following factors?
explanation: The correct answer is E.

question_text:
A patient is overdosed with a drug that is weakly acidic with a pKa of 5.5 and is a pglycoprotein substrate. Which of the following scenarios would MOST LIKELY
increase the renal drug excretion?

options.text: pH of urine is increased to 7.5
pH of urine is decreased to 5.0

explanation: The correct answer is C.

question_text:
(µg/
L)
The attached graph shows pharmacokinetic data of a patient taking drug M for
epilepsy. Which of the following statements BEST describes the attached graph?

options.text: Concurrent administration of a selective serotonin reuptake inhibitor will reduce the minimum toxic concentration of drug M
Grapefruit juice will reduce the plasma concentrations of drug M
Concurrent administration of carbamazepine will affect the minimum effective concentration of drug M
Ketoconazole will not affect the plasma concentrations of drug M
The mean steady-state concentration of drug M is about 150 µg/L

explanation: The correct answer is E.

question_text:
A 65-year-old woman was diagnosed to be “allergic to non-steroidal antiinflammatory drugs and nuts”. What is the MOST APPROPRIATE precaution for her
to adopt?

options.text: Take ginseng to tonify her qi
Carry a Medic Awas Card with her at all time

explanation: The correct answer is D.

question_text:
Which of the following statements BEST describes “Therapeutic Drug
Monitoring (TDM)”?

options.text: TDM is a yearly measurement of plasma concentrations of drugs to find out whether the drug levels are within the therapeutic range
TDM is a monthly measurement of plasma concentrations of drugs to find out whether the drug levels are within the therapeutic range
TDM is to measure the peak and the trough concentrations of drugs
TDM is to determine plasma concentrations of drugs in order to assist in clinical decision of patient management
TDM is to measure the bioequivalence of therapeutic drugs.

explanation: The correct answer is D.

question_text:
The attached figure represents graded concentration-responses of drugs
acting at the same receptor in the same tissue. If M represents the response to
a full agonist alone, which of the following statements BEST describes L?

options.text: L represents M in the presence of an irreversible antagonist
L represents M in the presence of an allosteric antagonist
L represents an inverse agonist alone
L represents a partial agonist alone
L represents a competitive antagonist alone

explanation: The correct answer is E.

question_text:
The attached figure represents a quantal-dose response for Drug X. Which of
the following statements BEST describes this quantal-dose response?

options.text: The EC50 for Drug X is 25 mg/day
The ED50 for Drug X is between 10 mg/day and 20 mg/day
The response measured is an all-or-none clinical response
An individual patient will have a greater response to 25 mg/day than 5 mg/day of Drug X
Fewer patients show clinical improvement on 35 mg/day than 25 mg/day of Drug X

explanation: The correct answer is C.

question_text:
Which of the following is LEAST LIKELY to be an appropriate route for the
administration of a local anaesthetic?
explanation: The correct answer is A.

question_text:
A 40-year-old woman without significant medical history was given general
anaesthesia by sevoflurane for uterine fibroid removal. She developed
hypertension, tachycardia, generalised skeletal muscle rigidity and marked
hyperthermia. What is the MOST LIKELY mechanism responsible for this rare
complication of anaesthesia?

options.text: Increase in muscle cell calcium levels
Activation of brain dopamine receptors by sevoflurane
Release of acetylcholine at nerve junctions in the skeletal muscle
Active metabolites of the sevoflurane activating glutamate receptors

explanation: The correct answer is A.

question_text: Which of the following is FALSE regarding arachidonic acid metabolites?

options.text: They may contribute to the pathogenesis of pain
They can act as chemotactic factors

explanation: The correct answer is C.

question_text:
A 16-year-old female presents with severe menstrual pain and was prescribed
Arcoxia, a COX-2 selective inhibitor. Which one of the following will Arcoxia
inhibit?

options.text: Synthesis of arachidonic acid synthesis from linoleic acid
Release of arachidonic acid from membrane phospholipids

explanation: The correct answer is D.

question_text:
Dr T has noticed that some of his patients who are being treated with a new
antidepressant appear to be ‘non-responsive' to the drug. He sent their blood
samples for testing and discovered that the drug concentrations in the plasma
were very low even though the metabolic products of the drug were in
abundance. The drug is generally well-absorbed following oral administration,
is eliminated primarily by hepatic metabolism through CYP2D6, and has an
elimination half-life of about 17 hours. What is the MOST LIKELY reason for
the patients' lack of response to the drug?

options.text: Non-compliance of the patient to dosing instructions
Food interaction whereby the drug is chelated and prevented from being absorbed
Many Chinese patients have slower activity of the CYP2D6 enzyme

explanation: The correct answer is E.

question_text:
The doctor is discharging his patient for follow up at the outpatient clinic. He
writes a prescription as part of the discharge procedures. Who is the
prescription addressed to?

options.text: The patient's care giver at home

explanation: The correct answer is E.

question_text:
You have used intravenous (I/V) midazolam and I/V fentanyl to sedate a patient
before endoscopy. Which of the following is LEAST expected to happen to the
patient?
explanation: The correct answer is E.

question_text:
A 35-year-old lady was diagnosed with systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE)
and was treated with prednisone. Which of the following is she LEAST LIKELY
to develop as a side-effect?
explanation: The correct answer is C.

question_text:
Prednisolone is an effective anti-inflammatory agent for treating asthma
exacerbation. Which of the following is NOT a mechanism of action of
prednisolone?
explanation: The correct answer is C.

question_text:
A 75-year-old man with a history of chronic hypertension and benign prostatic
hyperplasia is prescribed prazosin. Which of the following adverse effects
should you highlight to this patient upon starting treatment?
explanation: The correct answer is A.

question_text:
Which of the following is a direct muscarinic receptor agonist used for the
treatment of urinary retention?
explanation: The correct answer is A.

question_text:
A 37-year-old woman presents with a mild allergic reaction. She is prescribed
a first-generation antihistamine drug that is also a muscarinic receptor
antagonist. Which of the following adverse effects is MOST LIKELY caused by
the muscarinic antagonism of this drug?
explanation: The correct answer is C.

question_text:
A 22-year-old woman bought chlorphreniramine over the counter for her “flu”.
Which of the following is the MOST LIKELY symptom if you examine her one
day after treatment?
explanation: The correct answer is A.

question_text:
An 82-year-old woman presents with pain at her synovial joints. She has a history
of peptic ulcer, acute myocardial infarction, and mild cognitive impairment. Which
of the following non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs would be MOST
APPROPRIATE for analgesia in this patient?
explanation: The correct answer is E.

question_text:
A 33-year-old woman was diagnosed as having early rheumatoid arthritis. Her
joint pain has been effectively managed with prescription of celecoxib, a nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drug (NSAID). She subsequently becomes pregnant.
Which of the statements BEST describes the use of NSAIDs during pregnancy?

options.text: All NSAIDs are contraindicated in the third trimester of pregnancy
Naproxen is preferable to celecoxib during pregnancy
Paracetamol can be safely used to effectively control joint inflammation during pregnancy
Aspirin is preferable to celecoxib during pregnancy
The patient should continue to take celecoxib throughout her pregnancy

explanation: The correct answer is A.

question_text:
A 75-year-old man with rheumatoid arthritis is prescribed celecoxib. Which of
the following is NOT an adverse effect of celecoxib?

options.text: Increased risk of bruising and bleeding

explanation: The correct answer is B.

question_text:
An immunocompromised patient presented with fever. Choose the BEST
statement.

options.text: Histoplasma capsulatum exists as a yeast form in tissue
Mucor infections are associated with travel to South America
Malassezia furfur is diagnosed with the ‘India ink’ stain
A CSF antigen test is available for Penicillium marneffei
Diagnosis of Pneumocystis jiroveci is confirmed by culture

explanation: The correct answer is A.

question_text:
Which of the following diseases is NOT associated with a virus that exploits
host neuronal cells in the processes of viral latency and reactivation?
explanation: The correct answer is D.

question_text: Which of the following is NOT a feature of influenza viruses?

options.text: Certain antiviral agents can inhibit the viral neuraminidase
They are enveloped viruses, and vaccines are produced in hen’s eggs
Different subtypes are classified based on their haemagglutinin (H) and nucleoprotein (N), e.g. H1N1
They can undergo antigenic drift and antigenic shift
Some infected individuals are susceptible to bacterial superinfections, e.g. Streptococcus pneumoniae

explanation: The correct answer is C.

question_text: Which of the following concerning prions is FALSE?

options.text: Can be transmitted between different animal species

explanation: The correct answer is E.

question_text: The recommended diagnostic techniques for hepatic amoebiasis are:
explanation: The correct answer is C.

question_text: Correct and timely diagnosis for malaria is critical because:

options.text: Untreated infections are almost always fatal
Severity of infection and treatment varies with the infecting species
Patients rapidly deteriorate upon admission to hospital
There are very few options for treatment if diagnosis is delayed
Malaria symptoms are similar to less severe diseases

explanation: The correct answer is B.

question_text:
A 19-year-old national serviceman was suspected to have infectious
mononucleosis based on his history and clinical findings. Infectious
mononucleosis:

options.text: May be caused by mumps virus
Is also known as glanders fever
Is associated with human herpes virus type 7
Is characterized by the presence of atypical eosinophils

explanation: The correct answer is D.

question_text: The recommendation for routine laboratory diagnosis of Giardia infection is?
explanation: The correct answer is B.

question_text:
It is important to identify Plasmodium (malaria) organisms to the species level
because:

options.text: More details are better for documentation
It is useful for epidemiological analysis
Diagnosis of P. falciparum infection constitutes a medical emergency
Diagnosis of P. vivax infection necessitates treatment of liver stages
Diagnosis of P. falciparum infection constitutes a medical emergency and diagnosis of P. vivax infection necessitates treatment of liver stages

explanation: The correct answer is E.

question_text:
The attached picture shows dark film microscopy of organisms found in a
painful penile ulcer. What is the MOST appropriate treatment?
explanation: The correct answer is B.

question_text:
The attached picture shows deep purple coloured cocci in clusters, in a blood
culture taken from a 34-year-old man who was admitted in respiratory distress.
He had been well until two days earlier. On admission he was confused,
febrile, and had an aortic valve murmur. Endocarditis was suspected. A day
later the organism was reported as a methicillin susceptible Staphylococcus
aureus. Select the BEST answer for this patient’s story:

options.text: The tube coagulase test on S. aureus would normally be negative
Ignore the S. aureus, as it is likely to be a contaminant in this scenario
Endocarditis is found in 20-30% of patients with S. aureus bacteraemia
Treat him with vancomycin and optimize dosing, it is preferred to cloxacillin
Treat him for viridans streptococci, they are the main cause of endocarditis

explanation: The correct answer is C.

question_text:
The attached picture shows a spirochaete. It was prepared directly from a
genital chancre, due to syphilis. Select the BEST answer.

options.text: This is a picture of an immunofluorescent stain
Treponema pallidum grows after 3-5 days on blood agar
Syphilis specific serology reflects disease activity
Penicillin is not reliable for the treatment of syphilis

explanation: The correct answer is B.

question_text:
The attached image shows Gram-positive cocci that cause beta-haemolysis on
blood agar. Pharyngitis due to this organism should be treated with which of
the following regimens?
explanation: The correct answer is C.

question_text:
A patient arrived from the Indian subcontinent recently, and presented with
profuse watery diarrhoea. Gram stain of bacteria isolated from stool culture
showed a Gram-negative bacterium (see attached picture). What is the MOST
appropriate treatment?
explanation: The correct answer is C.

question_text:
The attached picture shows a large worm, several centimetres long. Select the
BEST answer.

options.text: Nematodes are commonly referred to as ‘flukes’
Hookworms are typically several centimetres long
Dracunculus medinensis is known as the ‘Guinea worm’
Schistosomiasis causes skin lesions known as ‘larva currens’
Mature helminths are found in the intermediate host

explanation: The correct answer is C.

question_text:
The attached image is a Gram stain of a blood culture showing purple cocci in
chains. Which of the following is the MOST accurate statement?

options.text: In a five-year-old child with meningitis, this is likely to be Escherichia coli
In an adult with pyelonephritis, this is likely to be Streptococcus pyogenes
Treat with vancomycin, as it may be methicillin resistant Staphylococcus aureus
Treatment should include a penicillin, as it may be Listeria monocytogenes
In a one-day-old baby with sepsis, this is likely to be Streptococcus agalactiae

explanation: The correct answer is E.

question_text:
The attached picture was reported as Cryptococcus neoformans. The stained
image was prepared from the CSF of a 58-year-old man who was investigated
for persistent headache of recent onset. Select the BEST answer.

options.text: A CSF antigen test is available for Cryptococcus neoformans
This is the typical appearance of Cryptococcus on a Gram stain
Confirm with an antigen test as Candida albicans can appear like this
Cryptococcus is often associated with repeated antibiotic courses
Supplement cultures with oil as Cryptococcus is a lipophilic yeast

explanation: The correct answer is A.

question_text:
A 5-year-old boy presented with high fever, headache and vomiting. The
attached image shows the Gram stain smear of his cerebrospinal fluid sample.
Which of the following antibiotics are appropriate for initial empiric therapy?
explanation: The correct answer is D.

question_text:
Which of the following statements regarding the laboratory diagnosis of HIV is
TRUE?

options.text: A negative anti-HIV antibody test excludes the likelihood that a patient is infected with HIV
It is mandatory to confirm a positive anti-HIV antibody result by different assays
Cell culture of HIV is routinely performed to determine antiviral sensitivity
The presence of anti-HIV antibodies in a 2-month-old baby indicates that vertical transmission of infection has occurred
Anti-HIV antibodies disappear in late-stage disease, giving rise to a falsenegative test result

explanation: The correct answer is B.

question_text:
The attached picture shows a microscopic image of Talaromyces marneffei
(previously called Penicillium marneffei). Select the BEST statement.

options.text: Talaromyces marneffei produces a brown pigment
Talaromyces marneffei is associated with South America
Talaromyces marneffei typically affects immunocompetent people
Talaromyces marneffei is classified as a “superficial” infection
Talaromyces marneffei is a thermally dimorphic fungus

explanation: The correct answer is E.

question_text:
Concerning malaria, microscopic examination of Giemsa-stained blood films
is NOT routinely employed to determine:

options.text: Species of malaria parasites infecting the patient

explanation: The correct answer is B.

question_text:
In Southeast Asia, a recognized zoonotic malaria-causing species from
non-human primates is:
explanation: The correct answer is C.

question_text:
An AIDS patient is on the HAART regimen that includes zidovudine. Which
drug is MOST LIKELY to cause additive anaemia and neutropenia if it is
added to the regimen?
explanation: The correct answer is C.

question_text:
Which of the following statements about "once-daily" dosing with
aminoglycosides is NOT accurate?

options.text: Dosage adjustment is less important in renal insufficiency
It is convenient for outpatient therapy
It is often less toxic than conventional (multiple) dosing regimens
Less nursing time is required for drug administration
Underdosing is less of a problem

explanation: The correct answer is A.

question_text:
A doctor suffered from a needle-stick injury while treating a HIV-positive
patient. Following a risk and benefit assessment, the doctor was
recommended to receive post-exposure prophylaxis (PEP) against HIV.
Which of the following statements regarding PEP is TRUE?

options.text: A combination of two anti-retroviral drugs will need to be taken daily by the doctor for the treatment period
PEP needs to be taken within the first 24 hours of exposure
A combination of three anti-retroviral drugs will need to be taken daily by the doctor for the treatment period
The doctor will not experience any adverse effects due to the antiretroviral drugs as he is not immunocompromised

explanation: The correct answer is C.

question_text:
A 24-year-old female cut herself and developed a hot and painful abscess on
her arm. What is the single BEST answer?

options.text: Staphylococcus saprophyticus is the most likely cause
Vibrio vulnificus is a likely cause of skin abscesses
Oral cloxacillin should cover the most likely organisms
S. aureus is found in the nares of 50% of the population
Incision and debridement are unhelpful in superficial abscesses

explanation: The correct answer is C.

question_text:
A 58-year-old man presented with a skin lesion shown in the attached picture.
Select the BEST statement.

options.text: This appearance would be unusual for a dermatophyte infection
Dermatophytes can be identified by characteristic macroconidia
Superficial fungal infections are transmitted from foods of animal origin
Serology is a useful method for diagnosing dermatophyte infections
A dimorphic fungus causes two shapes, a ring and a central spot

explanation: The correct answer is B.

question_text: Which of the following tumour – tumour marker pairs is CORRECT?
explanation: The correct answer is C.

question_text:
Which of the following is the MOST APPROPRIATE example of a carcinoma
in-situ?

options.text: A small leiomyoma of the uterus
Tubular adenoma with low-grade dysplasia of the rectum
Follicular carcinoma confined to the thyroid

explanation: The correct answer is B.

question_text:
A high rate of cell proliferation in cancer cells requires synthesis of
nucleotides, fatty acids and other cellular components. Metabolically, this
leads to a high demand for
explanation: The correct answer is D.

question_text:
Which of the following viruses is MOST strongly implicated as a causative
agent of cancer in humans?
explanation: The correct answer is E.

question_text: Which of the following is the immediate precursor to invasive carcinoma?
explanation: The correct answer is A.

question_text:
Which of the following statements regarding the features that distinguish
a cancer cell from a normal cell is MOST accurate?

options.text: Cancer cells sustain inactivating mutation of the proto-oncogene whereas normal cells usually shows activating mutation of the tumour suppressor gene
Cancer cells can greatly exceed the Hayflick's limit by inactivation of telomerase enzyme
Cancer cells can undergo a metabolic switch to aerobic glycolysis for rapid growth
Cancer cells have more stable genome than normal cells
Cancer cells activate apoptosis whereas normal cells usually do not undergo apoptosis

explanation: The correct answer is C.

question_text: Which of the following is a LIKELY attribute of cancer?

options.text: Downregulation of growth factor receptor expression

explanation: The correct answer is A.

question_text:
Which of the following is the LEAST accurate statement about
chemotherapy?

options.text: Chemotherapy agents have greater selectivity against proliferating cells
Chemotherapy agents primarily function through inducing apoptosis
Some chemotherapy agents inhibit microtubule formation
Some chemotherapy agents inhibit DNA replication
Chemotherapy has a broad therapeutic window between effective treatment of cancer cells and normal tissue toxicity

explanation: The correct answer is E.

question_text:
A cancer patient was treated with high-dose chemotherapy 8 days ago. He is
now febrile and you suspect an infection has developed. His absolute
neutrophil count is 200 cells per microlitre. Immune defence against
which of the following microbes is MOST LIKELY to be compromised in
this individual?
explanation: The correct answer is C.

question_text:
Specimens sent for urine culture should reach the laboratory within what
maximum time period after collection?
explanation: The correct answer is C.

question_text:
A 43-year-old man with asymptomatic hyperuricaemia is about to start
therapy for newly diagnosed essential hypertension. Which of the following
antihypertensive drugs is MOST LIKELY to increase his serum uric acid levels
further, and possibly precipitate a gout attack?
explanation: The correct answer is C.

question_text:
A 56-year-old male patient with a history of hypertension and urinary
hesitancy due to benign prostatic hyperplasia is transferred to your
clinic. He claims that both conditions were previously treated by use of a
single drug. You suspect this drug would MOST LIKELY be:
explanation: The correct answer is C.

question_text:
Which ONE of the following pharmacological groups of diuretic agents acts
preferentially on the collecting duct of the nephron?
explanation: The correct answer is D.

question_text:
A 60-year-old man with severe renal impairment and type 2 diabetes
mellitus is diagnosed with severe invasive candidiasis. Which of the
following drugs would be MOST suitable for systemic antifungal
treatment of this patient?
explanation: The correct answer is D.

question_text:
Urinary tract infection (UTI) is a common cause of sepsis. Which one of the
following statements BEST describes this condition?

options.text: Escherichia coli has more endotoxin than other Enterobacteriaceae
Only Gram-negative bacteria cause sepsis and multi-organ failure
Pseudomonas aeruginosa is a common cause of hospital-associated UTI
Clindamycin is used in UTI-associated sepsis to reduce production of toxin
Staphylococcus lugdunensis is associated with UTI in young women

explanation: The correct answer is C.

question_text:
Uropathogenic E. coli has a structure (see arrow in the attached image) which
allows it to attach to uroepithelial cells. What is the structure?
explanation: The correct answer is B.

question_text:
A 32-year-old man presented with renal failure. A biopsy of his kidney showed
tubular epithelial death, while glomeruli remained largely normal. Regarding
his condition, which of the following is MOST CORRECT?

options.text: This is the most common cause of acute kidney injury
There is most likely an adjacent renal tumour
Renal artery stenosis is a likely cause
This is a form of glomerulonephritis

explanation: The correct answer is B.

question_text:
Electron microscopy (EM) is a useful adjunct diagnostic technique to evaluate
the nature and cause of glomerular disease. What is the MOST suitable
fixative that the tissue biopsy should be placed in, to facilitate optimal EM
assessment to take place?

options.text: No need fixative; fresh tissue will suffice

explanation: The correct answer is C.

question_text:
A 72-year-old man with long-standing poorly controlled diabetes mellitus
was diagnosed with end-stage renal failure. Which of the following
electrolyte imbalances is he MOST at risk of developing?
explanation: The correct answer is C.

question_text:
A 48-year-old woman was admitted to the emergency department with
acute pulmonary oedema. She was prescribed furosemide. Which of the
following statements regarding furosemide is CORRECT?

options.text: It acts mainly at the distal tubule
It has a slow onset and prolonged diuretic action
It selectively inhibits the Na+/Cl- transporter
It can be used for treatment of acute hyperkalaemia
It can be used for treatment of nephrogenic diabetes insipidus

explanation: The correct answer is D.

question_text: Which of the following statements about spironolactone is CORRECT?

options.text: It blocks aldosterone receptors that are responsible for volume regulation
It has a fast onset and long duration of action
It may induce hypokalaemia with the long-term treatment
It may induce metabolic alkalinisation when used to treat chronic heart failure
It produces increased facial hair growth in women

explanation: The correct answer is A.

question_text:
A 4-year-old child presented with a 4-month history of a gradually
enlarging abdominal mass (more prominent on the right side), associated
with poor appetite and stagnant body weight. Subsequent imaging
studies established the presence of a right kidney mass for which
nephrectomy was performed. The pathological features are shown in the
attached figure. What is MOST well- known regarding the natural history
of this tumour?

options.text: Indolent growth and confined to the affected kidney
Metastasizes to para-aortic lymph nodes, but not elsewhere
Metastasizes to lungs, liver and brain
Often develops paraneoplastic syndromes, but does not metastasize

explanation: The correct answer is C.

question_text:
A 31-year-old man presented with renal failure. Genetic studies revealed
a mutation in his PKD1 gene. Which of the following would you expect to
find in this patient?
explanation: The correct answer is B.

question_text: Which of the following patients is at highest risk of developing urolithiasis?

options.text: A 31-year-old woman with vitamin C deficiency
A 42-year-old man with high urinary pH values
A 64-year-old man with an indwelling urinary catheter

explanation: The correct answer is C.

question_text:
A 4-year-old child was found to be oedematous and lethargic. Laboratory
findings included evidence of proteinuria and lipiduria, low serum
albumin levels, and hyperlipidaemia. A renal biopsy led to the diagnosis
of minimal change disease. What is LIKELY to be the expected electron
microscopic finding?
explanation: The correct answer is B.

question_text:
A 19-year-old soldier completed a 50-km road march in the sun, and
collapsed shortly after. He was found to be severely dehydrated. Which of
the following renal conditions is he LIKELY to suffer from?
explanation: The correct answer is A.

question_text:
Which of the following statements BEST describes the general mechanism by
which hydrochlorothiazide causes hypokalaemia?

options.text: Action as an aldosterone receptor agonist, thereby favouring K+ loss
Block of proximal tubular ATP-dependent secretory K+ pumps
Increased distal tubular Na+ reabsorption in exchange for K+
Inhibition of proximal tubular Na+/K+-ATPase such that K+ is actively pumped into the urine
Lowering of distal tubular urine osmolality, thereby favouring passive diffusion of K+ into the urine

explanation: The correct answer is C.

question_text: After a healthy individual runs 20 km in hot weather without drinking,

options.text: peritubular capillary hydrostatic pressures will be lower than before exercise
intracellular fluid volume will be increased
plasma anti-diuretic hormone (ADH) will be reduced
a respiratory acidosis is likely to be observed

explanation: The correct answer is A.

question_text: ECF osmolality will decrease

options.text: after drinking one litre of a 260 mOsm/L solution
after intravenous infusion of isotonic saline
when the thoracic duct is obstructed
when plasma urea levels are elevated

explanation: The correct answer is B.

question_text:
In the presence of anti-diuretic hormone (ADH), the greatest fraction of filtered
water is absorbed in the
explanation: The correct answer is A.

question_text:
As a result of countercurrent exchange, the fluid in the tubule is most
hyperosmotic in the

options.text: ascending limb of loop of Henle
descending limb of loop of Henle
loop of Henle in the inner medulla

explanation: The correct answer is E.

question_text: Dilation of efferent arterioles results in an increase in

options.text: hydrostatic pressure in the space of Bowman’s capsule

explanation: The correct answer is A.

question_text: Why would eating a very salty snack make you thirsty?

options.text: It would cause decreased ECF volume.
It would decrease the osmolarity of the ECF.
It would inhibit the medullary chemoreceptors.
It would stimulate the hypothalamic osmoreceptors.
It would immediately cause more urine output.

explanation: The correct answer is D.

question_text:
A patient has severe renal artery stenosis (narrowing). Administration of an
Angiotensin Converting Enzyme inhibitor (ACE inhibitor) to the above
patient might lead to acute renal failure by

options.text: increasing renal resistance to blood flow
causing plasma proteins to be excreted in the urine

explanation: The correct answer is E.

question_text:
In the presence of aldosterone, the greatest fraction of filtered sodium
is absorbed in the
explanation: The correct answer is A.

question_text: Which one of the following results in loss of isotonic fluid from the body?
explanation: The correct answer is A.

question_text:
A healthy individual donated a kidney for transplantation. What is MOST
LIKELY to happen to his threshold for glucose reabsorption after
donation?
explanation: The correct answer is D.

question_text:
In an untreated patient with congestive heart failure, the finding of a low
plasma sodium concentration is MOST LIKELY due to

options.text: increased production of atrial natriuretic factor

explanation: The correct answer is B.

question_text:
In a posterior approach to carry out a biopsy of the right kidney, the nerve
MOST LIKELY to be injured is the
explanation: The correct answer is D.

question_text:
A 50-year-old male patient has chronic renal failure and requires regular
kidney dialysis. The Hb is 10g/dL (13.1-16.6). Which one of the following will
be a characteristic feature of this patient?

options.text: The serum phosphate level is low.
The anaemia is typically microcytic hypochromic.

explanation: The correct answer is D.

question_text:
Which one of the following is MOST LIKELY to be found in a patient with
metabolic acidosis?

options.text: Stimulation of central chemoreceptors in the medulla
High affinity of Hb for O2

explanation: The correct answer is B.

question_text:
As blood flows from the afferent to efferent arteriole the net glomerular
filtration pressure
explanation: The correct answer is B.

question_text: The glomerular filtration rate (GFR) will increase in response to

options.text: increased Cl- delivery to the macula densa.

explanation: The correct answer is E.

question_text: A substance has clearance less than that of inulin because of

options.text: neither secretion nor reabsorption in the tubules.
binding to proteins in the tubules.
higher secretion in the proximal tubule than the distal tubules.

explanation: The correct answer is A.

question_text: In a patient with uncompensated metabolic alkalosis,

options.text: plasma pH, plasma HCO3- and arterial PCO2 are all low.
plasma HCO3- and arterial PCO2 are low but plasma pH is high.
plasma pH and HCO3- are low but arterial PCO2 is normal.
plasma pH and HCO3- are high but arterial PCO2 is normal.
plasma pH is low, HCO3- is high but arterial PCO2 is normal.

explanation: The correct answer is D.

question_text: Glomerular filtration rate (GFR)

options.text: is the main determinant of the rate of urine production.
is directly proportional to the systemic arterial blood pressure.
is significantly reduced upon a drop in mean arterial blood pressure below 60 mmHg.
increases with IgA deposits in the glomerulus.

explanation: The correct answer is C.

question_text: Maintenance of K+ homeostasis is under the direct influence of
explanation: The correct answer is C.

question_text:
A patient presents with the following arterial blood composition:
Two days later the arterial blood composition is:
Which one of the following BEST reflects these analyses?

options.text: The first blood sample was within normal limits.
There is renal compensation from the time of the first to the second blood sample.

explanation: The correct answer is D.

question_text:
The osmotic pressure of plasma is higher than the interstitial fluids because
of
explanation: The correct answer is C.

question_text:
Which one of the following leads to the greatest reduction of the net filtration
pressure?

options.text: Low hydrostatic pressure in the Bowman’s capsule
High hydrostatic pressure in the glomerulus

explanation: The correct answer is C.

question_text: Isotonic saline infusion will result in
explanation: The correct answer is B.

question_text:
Which one of the following statements regarding phosphate regulation is
TRUE?

options.text: Its absorption by the gut is increased by calcitonin.
Its levels in plasma tend to be high in hyperparathyroidism.
Its reabsorption by the renal tubules is increased by calcitonin.
Its reabsorption by the renal tubules is not affected by parathyroid hormone.
Its reabsorption by renal tubules is reduced by parathyroid hormone.

explanation: The correct answer is E.

question_text:
In a kidney, ureter and bladder (KUB) X-ray, the ureter can be traced by
following the

options.text: spinous processes of the thoracic vertebrae.
lateral edge of the quadratus lumborum.
transverse processes of the lower lumbar vertebrae.
medial edge of the psoas major.

explanation: The correct answer is C.

question_text:
After a period of strenuous exercise, Tim consumed a large amount of water
in an attempt to relieve his thirst. How would this action affect the balance of
fluids and electrolytes in his body?

options.text: No effect because intake of water will counter water lost during the exercise.
No effect because physiological systems will act immediately to counter the changes.
Increased volume in the extracellular fluid (ECF) will cause water to move out from cells into the ECF.
Increased volume in the ECF will cause water to move from the ECF into cells.
Increased volume in the ECF will cause sodium ions to move out of cells due to dilution of the concentration of these ions in the ECF

explanation: The correct answer is D.

question_text:
During a routine check-up, a 15-year-old girl was found to have a
horseshoe kidney. Which of the following complications is she at risk for
developing?
explanation: The correct answer is A.
